claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I have the most accurate information for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children under 20 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 14.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have Alex who is 8 years old and Blake who is 14 years old.

Now I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do both Alex and Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods, or receiving any benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care, and they don't receive any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 8):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (age 14):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**Important information:**
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, so you'll be the claimant for both children.
- Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
- When Blake turns 16, you can continue to receive Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, up until they turn 20.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application for both children!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex does not receive benefits in their own right. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex as they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK.

Blake is 14 years old, which is under 16. Blake lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Blake. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Blake does not receive benefits in their own right. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Blake as they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for two children (Alex, age 8, and Blake, age 14) who both live with them. Both children are under 16 years old. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, and neither receives benefits in their own right. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
